title: Constant-time string compare
prompt_md:
Comparing secrets (password hashes, token signatures) with `==` is dangerous: `==` can short-circuit at the first differing character, and an attacker can measure those tiny timing differences to recover the secret byte by byte. This is a **timing attack**.

Implement `safe_equal(a, b)` → `bool`, using `hmac.compare_digest`, which always examines the full input and returns in (near) constant time.
hints: `hmac.compare_digest(a, b)` does exactly this — wrap and return it.
